comment.body:
their not though 
sentiment on X is bullish 
what I would argue is that the pullback we see will be relatively mild and not retest April lows $480 on SPY 
but be somewhere along the lines of $530-540 range 

rationale being that everyone is fully hedged for May OPEX cause of what we saw in April
which should protect against any significant downside moves and allow MMs to keep all the option premiums as they expire worthless

comment.body: upside pivot was 5684.56 according to the intraday post, market closed above it so I assume short set up is falsified and grind towards 5720 Monday. The high of First half hour of RTH session got defended during rest of day, which signals bullish strength. The whole wick of Thursday's ON-RTH got filled and price closed above confirming momo strength. Some negative news hit the tape wrt peace talks with Russia but that didn't move the needle much. Positve gamma positions increased enormously Thursday-Friday. Look i'm 1/20 of the brain of Alma and i'd love to hear nuanced bearporn during the weekend but when it runs like a bull...

comment.body:
Alma mentioned May 2 as an important catalyst. 
There was no announcement about the minimis for a pause or something else yesterday. No supportive messages for SMB's.  
Nothing has really changed since liberation day on April 7. 
China still demands that Trump abolishes all tariffs  imposed on them, before they negotiate. 
Also no peace deals have been made yet. 
The markets are frontrunning a lot while nothing has changed or seems to change yet. 
Interesting times. 
I am anxious to know what Alma's read is of above.
